IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Following a customer request we will be upgrading a transformer and overhead electricity cables for a larger electricity supply. Once completed this will provide the customer with their desired outcome as we have a duty to provide electricity when requested. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we complete the work safely. To enable our engineers to be able to upgrade the low voltage network that the generator is connected to, we will need to switch off your power for a longer period on the 11th June 2026 to ensure they can carry out their works safely. Once the planned work on the 11th June 2026 has been completed you will be re-connected back to our electricity network. There will be a road closure on Somersham Road on the 10th and 11th June 2026 and three way traffic management on Loraine Way (B1113) at the junction with The Street on 11th June 2026 for the works to be completed safely.  This work is taking place in the Somersham Road and Loraine Way

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area.Our contractors Ground Control need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 75 metres across 1 sections of the network. Due to the scale of the work, the power interruption is 5 hours and 30 minutes. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages as we appreciate your area has been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable. This work is taking place off Tysea Hill.

Hi, this is a message from Donna at UK Power Networks with an important update regarding the planned power cut that was scheduled for today the 8th June 2026 in the TN21 postcode area.
Our engineers have advised that due an issue with the third party tree surgeons the works cannot go ahead so the planned power cut has been cancelled.
These works may be re-scheduled to a later date and affected customers will be notified nearer the time.
Our reference number for this was INCD-102119-V.
We would like to apologise for any inconvenience this may cause you. If you have any questions or you have moved from the TN21 postcode area, then please call 105. Thank you.

Steps.Message: Good afternoon, this is a message from Harry at UK Power Networks regarding the planned power cut which is currently in progress today the 8th June 2026 in the IP23 postcode area. The power was switched off to enable a third party to trim trees that had grown close to the overhead electricity cables near Hestley Green. Our engineers have advised us that the 9 affected properties will be restored early between approximately 14:10 and 14:30. This is due to the works progressing well and being completed ahead of schedule. Once these works are complete this will improve the reliability of the electricity network and reduce the risk of you having a tree related unplanned power cut. Our reference number for this work is INCD-109070-C. I hope that the power coming on earlier is of benefit to you. We always aim to provide a 10/10 service, if you feel we have not delivered you a 10/10 service then please call us on 105.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: A third party has arranged for trees near the overhead electricity lines to be trimmed and has asked us to temporarily switch off the power so this can be carried out safely. Some of these trees have grown close to the cables and could cause damage, particularly during poor weather. Allowing this work to take place helps keeps the network safe and reliable. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut trees away from the overhead cables. This work will help reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts in the future as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, the power interruption is approximately 6 hours.  This work is taking place in the Hestley Green area.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified 7 poles that need replacing and upgrading. We will also be replacing a switch on the high voltage network. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the poles and replace the equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. This work is taking place on Goodbury Road. A road closure will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Sent by Talie at 07:32 - Our engineers have restored power to all 443 properties in the Tring, HP23 6 and Chesham, HP5 2 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around the overhead cable fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

Sent by Talie at 07:09 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an overhead cable fault that initially interrupted power to 443 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 248 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 09:30-10:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.

What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 16:07 - Our engineers have restored power to all 244 properties in the Kings Lynn PE33/PE34 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have now diverted power around the overhead network fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Aaron at 15:42 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an overhead network fault that initially interrupted power to 244 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 183 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 17:00-18:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

We'll continue to keep you updated with our progress.
